mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects stronger labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in months with PAYEMS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or lag behind baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates stronger aggregate demand, which supports corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or underperform baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 310 signals elevated inflation, prompting monetary tightening expectations that depress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPI > 310 months are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or exceed baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with both INDPRO and GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or exhibit negative correlation with the combined proxies.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly FEDFUNDS rate above 5% indicates a tightening cycle, which raises borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in FEDFUNDS > 5% months are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or exceed baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tighter labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in UNRATE decline months are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or outperform baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects stronger demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in HOUST growth months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or underperform baseline returns.
